title: Pretrained Time-Series Foundation Models for Financial Return Forecasting
abstract: arXiv:2606.27100v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Financial return forecasting is a difficult test case for time-series foundation models (TSFMs) due to low signal-to-noise ratios, structural breaks, heavy tails, and weak persistence. This paper benchmarks pretrained TSFMs against train-from-scratch neural baselines in a deliberately conservative financial setting. We evaluate TimeGPT/TimeGPT-LH, TimesFM-2.5, Moirai-2.0, Chronos, and Chronos-2 against NBEATS, NHITS, PatchTST, iTransformer, and KAN on five liquid U.S. equities (AAPL, AMZN, GOOG, JPM, META) using linear and log returns. Models are compared under an equalized context budget, a rolling-origin protocol, and against random-walk benchmarks. We provide a theoretical framing of pretraining as an inductive prior, linking PAC-Bayes transfer intuition, information-theoretic predictability limits, and attention geometry. This clarifies why strong model rankings need not imply economically meaningful predictability in noisy markets.

Title: Pretrained Time-Series Foundation Models for Financial Return Forecasting
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27100v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Financial return forecasting is a difficult test case for time-series foundation models (TSFMs) due to low signal-to-noise ratios, structural breaks, heavy tails, and weak persistence. This paper benchmarks pretrained TSFMs against train-from-scratch neural baselines in a deliberately conservative financial setting. We evaluate TimeGPT/TimeGPT-LH, TimesFM-2.5, Moirai-2.0, Chronos, and Chronos-2 against NBEATS, NHITS, PatchTST, iTransformer, and KAN on five liquid U.S. equities (AAPL, AMZN, GOOG, JPM, META) using linear and log returns. Models are compared under an equalized context budget, a rolling-origin protocol, and against random-walk benchmarks. We provide a theoretical framing of pretraining as an inductive prior, linking PAC-Bayes transfer intuition, information-theoretic predictability limits, and attention geometry. translated_title: 用于金融收益预测的预训练时间序列基础模型
translated_abstract: 金融收益预测是时间序列基础模型（TSFMs）的一个困难测试案例，因为其具有低信噪比、结构性突变、重尾分布和弱持续性等特点。本文在刻意保守的金融环境中，将预训练的TSFMs与从零开始训练的神经基线模型进行了基准测试。我们评估了TimeGPT/TimeGPT-LH、TimesFM-2.5、Moirai-2.0、Chronos和Chronos-2，与NBEATS、NHITS、PatchTST、iTransformer和KAN在五种美国流动性股票（AAPL、AMZN、GOOG、JPM、META）上的线性和对数收益表现。模型在均衡上下文预算、滚动原点协议下进行比较，并与随机游走基准进行对比。我们提供了预训练作为归纳先验的理论框架，将PAC-Bayes迁移直觉、信息论可预测性限制和注意力几何联系起来。这澄清了为什么强模型排名不一定意味着在噪声市场中具有经济意义上的可预测性。
tldr: 本文在保守的金融环境中系统评估了多个预训练时间序列基础模型在金融收益预测上的表现，并理论解释了强模型排名不等于经济意义上可预测性的原因。
en_tdlr: This paper systematically evaluates multiple pretrained time-series foundation models for financial return forecasting in a conservative setting, and theoretically explains why strong model rankings do not imply economically meaningful predictability in noisy markets.
